item.title: How do you say "third-degree burn" in your language?
item.description: "Verbrennungen dritten Grades" (fur-BRAN-noong-an DREE-tunn GRAW-dis) "三度烧伤 san1 du4 shao1shang1" (sun do shao shung)
item.dc:creator: TheOneWhoWasLeft

item.title: Death of a Ukrainian poet
item.description:
Ukrainian poet and soldier, Maksym Kryvtsov, pictured here with his cat (about whom he wrote a poem), has been killed in action. His cat died too.
Here he is posing with his new poetry book called "Poems from the loophole". It was recognised by PEN Ukraine, an NGO protecting freedom of speech and authors' rights, as one of the best Ukrainian books of 2023. Rest in peace.
item.dc:creator: Huor

item.title: Got a jamon at Costco and been eating it for over a month
item.description:
Jamon slicing is harder than you'd think. This took about and hour
Jamon and eggs
Jamon on bagel
Jamon and congee
Jamon
Orzo carbonara with jamon
Jamon sammies pressed in a george foreman
Gnocchi with mushroom cream sauce and thinly shaved jamon
Jamon and egg tacos
Jamon and beer
Jamon on chips
Jamon pizza
Jamon and waffles
Jamon, egg, and cheese on bagel
Beans with jamon, cabbage with jamon, eggs without jamon
Cookie near jamon
Jamon and potato chowder
Jamon Big mac
Jamon, egg, and bean and jamon tacos
Jamon, mushroom, and green onion quiche
Jamon and olive easy mac
Jamon and French toast
Jamon penne alfredo
Jamon poutine
Jamon burger on toast
Jamon carnitas tacos
Jamon, fermented soy bean, and turnip green soup
Jamon rice bowl
Jamon dutch baby
Jamon chili dogs and japchae
Jamon cheesy toast
Jamon deviled eggs
Jamon, ham, and sides
Jamon and bean soup
Cheesecake near jamon
Jamon and ham fried rice
Jamon and ham omelette, jamon and ham hash
Jam on hamon on cheesy tomato toast
Jamon and corn chowder
Jamon, sour cream, and caviar on chips. The caviar was a gift. First time trying it. It tasted like shit. Literally, like fishy shit. Can't believe people buy this stuff
Jamon frites
Jamon and ham toastie
Chicken soup with rice and jamon
Kimchi fried rice with jamon
Green eggs and jamon
Minced jamon
Jamon on bagel
Jam on jamon on brie. @dogmeatsfood on the social things

item.title: Lock him up Already!
item.description: There are exactly 301 days until Decision 2024 This TRAITOR to America is looking to unleash Project 2025, f**k me it's some scary shit, check it out https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Project_2025 It's up to YOU to stop this dangerous march towards FASCISM! First things first .... register to vote / check your registration ... vote.org Secondly get involved, here are some handy links https://runforsomething.net/run/candidate-support-system/ https://www.turnoutpac.org/ https://justicedemocrats.com/ https://ballotpedia.org/Election_results,_2022:_Uncontested_races_by_state Why: https://www.axios.com/2023/11/13/trump-loyalists-2024-presidential-election Do your part today to protect DEMOCRACY!

item.title: Former trump DHS official, Miles Taylor, explains how he literally had to write highly classified national security memos at a first grade reading level so trump could understand them
item.description: Register to vote and/or check the status of your voter registration by going here: IWillVote.com Be sure to vote in every election, including the “small” ones. The “big” politicians often got their start by winning the small elections. You can help weed out the bad ones early. #vote

Have a good one :)

item.dc:creator: BossVarriack

item.title: It all started with a croissant
item.description:
A story of how something very small can become something very big.
The tiny island on which I live does not have a bakery that's open over the weekend. This is dumb, and mean, because bakeries are specifically FOR the weekend. So, about a year and a half ago, I decided to become the little engine that said "fuck it, I'll make my own damn croissants".
Spoiler alert: it did not go well. Neither did the next five tries, though they did get progressively less bad.
Finally, on the seventh try, I NAILED it. No, really, I did. Go check my post history, it was a real high point of my imagined internet fame. Also, if you ever decide to laminate dough by hand....maybe don't. Zero stars.
Unfortunately, this is a TINY island, which means word travels FAST, and rumors of the mythical mountain weekend pastry hermit began to travel. Armed with....absolutely nothing....villagers began to demand baked goods, threatening us by...offering payment and encouragement. Truly the stuff of nightmares.
And SO the "brunch box" was born. Every Sunday, we made about 20 boxes of assorted pastries and delivered them personally. I BLEW my damn kitchen up every. single. time. But even with the outrageous clean up, it was absolutely worth it. 10/10 would do again.
Over time, I got better and better, the villagers got fatter and fatter, and we got more and more popular. So, we eventually decided to "do this shit for reals", and in October of 2022 we took the actually terrifying step of buying a commercial space in which to build a bakery (that would be open on WEEKENDS). And then...
My brother died. He overdosed about a week before Christmas, and to find out more about that you can again look at my post history. His death made me feel a lot of things, and think a lot of things, as death often does. And ultimately, I realized that I don't particularly love making pastry, which is perhaps THE most unfortunate epiphany for a future bakery owner to have. What I do love, however, is cooking. Specifically cooking tiny, fancy, expensive food that internet generally hates. I like cooking lots of other foods too....tiny and fancy is just my area of expertise (both in men and in food, turns out). I came to understand that what I truly wanted to do was open a restaurant, but I was afraid that no one would want what I had to offer, and I questioned whether or not I was even good enough to be offering it in the first place. It's pretty scary to authentically put yourself out there and be open to judgement and even failture. And I had decided to try to avoid that big scary risk by hiding behind a croissant. If my brother's death has taught me anything, it's that our time here is too limited to waste any of it hiding. The best time to make a change is always now because now all we have. I'm fairly sure that's an Oasis lyric, and possibly also on a cat poster, but that CLEARLY only makes the statement more powerful. Get on board.
And that's how our brunch boxes turned into a supper club. For almost an entire year, we hosted 12 people in our home for dinner every Friday and Saturday evenings. And I made tiny fancy shit. And I loved it. And it turns out the villagers loved it too. As the supper clubs ran their course, we found people crazy enough to invest in our idea, which then made it possible to turn this:
With all of it's lovely...lime green and earth tones.....
Into this.
Where we make things like this. And so far, it's going pretty damn well. It's still ABsolutely fucking terrifying sometimes. But it's real, and honest, and worth it. 10/10 would do again. And as far as baking goes, I can't say that I miss it all that much. Because every Sunday morning....
We still do this. And now because of one little croissant we have a restaurant that's sometimes a bakery and is open on weekends.
item.dc:creator: 20somethingelse

item.title: Maybe I'm alone in this one, but I doubt that I am
item.description: There are several actors that I don't like but accept that they are good at what they do, even just moderately. There are those who carve out a niche for themselves and I can respect that. Seth Rogen isn't one of them. He only ever plays himself, he seems to have little to no emotional range and by my existence as a fan of comedy and animated movies, he has been inflicted upon me. He appears in, seemingly, everything; Invincible, The Super Mario Bros, Kung Fu Panda, as well as numerous live-action movies. In all of them, he plays himself. He can't do accents/different voices, he relies on his odd laugh (which is even the point of a joke in This is the End). Somehow, he consistently gets work. I mostly made this to break away from the political posts. Let's be honest, each day that puts us closer to November, this site is going to get more and more political as we each grow in fear of what will happen if we don't all vote. So, why not vent about someone whose career I cannot fathom?
item.dc:creator: LincLoud
